title: Functional Core, Imperative Shell, and Production Integration · Functional Design Board
summary: Independently repair and extend unfamiliar functional Python systems, trace lazy evaluation and closures, test claimed laws, and defend effect boundaries under changed constraints. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish value transformations from identity-changing commands in an execution trace.
Trace expression evaluation without relying on hidden statement order.
Predict mutation through shared aliases in nested Python containers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""python-functional immutable transformation portfolio."""

from collections.abc import Callable, Iterable, Iterator
from functools import partial, reduce
from itertools import chain, islice
from typing import TypeVar

T = TypeVar('T')
U = TypeVar('U')

# Extend one pure, lazy, tested pipeline increment per activity.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Value Model value-and-effect pipeline for this exam task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Value Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A local decision core receives an injected clock and storage callable. Replaying the same 3-event fixture must reproduce decisions while I/O failures stay at the imperative boundary.

content.text: Order work so Value Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
